question: Modify the `Book` class (from Q1) to include a default constructor (initializes with empty strings), a parameterized constructor, and a copy constructor. Demonstrate creating objects using all three types of constructors.

test_cases.expected:
Book 1 (Default): Title: N/A, Author: N/A
Book 2 (Parameterized): Title: The Great Gatsby, Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Book 3 (Copy of Book 2): Title: The Great Gatsby, Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
Book 4 (Copy of Book 1): Title: N/A, Author: N/A

errors: Default constructor initializes with 'N/A' instead of strictly 'empty strings' as requested, which is a deviation from specification.

Copy constructor takes its argument by value (`Book other`) instead of by const reference (`const Book& other`), leading to an unnecessary copy of the 'other' object and potential performance overhead, reducing robustness in resource management for large objects.
